channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump offers a public show of support for Pete Hegseth, his embattled nominee to lead the Pentagon
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump on Friday offered a public show of support for Pete Hegseth, his embattled choice to lead the Defense Department, whose confirmation by the Senate is in doubt as he faces questions over allegations of excessive drinking, sexual assault and his views on
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Johnson woos his sharpest critics in preparation for Speaker vote
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) is wooing his sharpest critics in the House GOP ahead of his official election for Speaker on the House floor, working to eliminate opposition and secure the strongest leadership mandate possible in a razor-thin majority. In the latest development, Rep.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Newsom visits border, slams Trump on planned deportations, tariffs
item.description: "No state will be more impacted by that, no region will be impacted by that, small businesses that will lose customers, jobs that will be lost, inflationary impacts on every single one of you," said Newsom.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Coroner says searchers think they have found missing Pennsylvania woman last seen near sinkhole
item.description: A Pennsylvania coroner's office said Friday that investigators believe they have located the body of a woman who was last seen four days earlier near a sinkhole above a shuttered coal mine.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sudan documentary implores world to remember how a hopeful revolution became a forgotten war
item.description: MARRAKECH, Morocco (AP) — In the early days of Sudan's 2019 revolution, Shajane Suliman brought sandwiches, coffee and mint tea to demonstrations in closed-off sections of Khartoum. But as hope made way for despair, she decided more than food was needed to nourish the movement.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Hunt for the gunman who killed UnitedHealthcare's CEO reveals new clues about movements in New York
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — As the hunt for a masked gunman who stalked and killed the head of the largest U.S. health insurer moved into its third day Friday, surveillance footage provided more clues about the suspect's travels and the places he visited before the shooting.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL BALSAMO, JAKE OFFENHARTZ and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Romania's top court annuls first round of presidential vote won by far-right candidate
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — A top Romanian court on Friday annulled the first round of the country's presidential election, days after allegations that Russia ran a coordinated online campaign to promote the far-right outside r who won the first round.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Judge urges NYC subway chokehold jury to keep deliberating after it struggles to reach verdict
item.description: The judge in Daniel Penny’s subway chokehold manslaughter trial has urged jurors to continue their deliberations after they told him they couldn’t reach a unanimous vote on the top charge.
item.dc:creator: Philip Marcelo, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Appeals court upholds TikTok ban law
item.description: A federal appeals court on Friday upheld a law requiring TikTok’s Chinese parent company to sell the popular app or face a U.S. ban.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, Fox 5

item.title: Thousands flee as Syrian insurgents advance to the doorstep of the country's third-largest city
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Thousands of people fled the central Syrian city of Homs, the country's third largest, as insurgents seized two towns on the outskirts Friday, positioning themselves for an assault on a potentially major prize in their march against President Bashar Assad.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Tyre Sampson's family awarded $310 million after fatal fall on Florida ride
item.description: Tyre Sampson, 14, was tragically killed after falling to his death off a freefall tower drop ride at Orlando’s ICON Park.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, WGN 9

item.title: Suspect in UnitedHealthcare CEO shooting used fake ID at hostel: sources
item.description: The man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson in Midtown is believed to have used a fake New Jersey ID to check into an Upper West Side hostel before the shooting, sources told Nexstar's WPIX.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Walz says he was 'a little surprised' by election loss
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D), Vice President Kamala Harris' running mate, reflected on the Democratic Party's loss in the presidential election, saying he was "a little surprised" by the outcome.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KRON4

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO's killing spotlights challenge of protecting leaders of companies
item.description: He’s one of the most famous and widely admired corporate leaders in the world. But it’s the haters that companies like Mark Zuckerberg’s Meta worry about.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Company fined $172K after children employed to clean Iowa pork plant
item.description: They employed 11 children to "use corrosive cleaners to clean head splitters, jaw pullers, bandsaws, neck clippers and other equipment.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FBI arrests Boston City Councilor Tania Fernandes Anderson on public corruption charges
item.description: Boston City Councilor Tania Fernandes Anderson was arrested Friday morning by FBI agents in Dorchester following a federal investigation, reports say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump nominates former border chief Rodney Scott as commissioner of CBP
item.description: President-elect Trump nominated former border chief Rodney Scott as the commissioner of U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP).
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: 7.0 earthquake off Northern California prompts brief tsunami warning
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A 7.0 magnitude earthquake shook a large area of Northern California on Thursday, knocking items off grocery store shelves, sending children scrambling under desks and prompting a brief tsunami warning for 5.3 million people along the U.S. West Coast.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: An Ole Miss student exchanged messages with the man now on trial in his killing, police say
item.description: In the final hours before University of Mississippi student Jimmy “Jay” Lee disappeared, sexually explicit Snapchat messages were exchanged between his account and the account of the man now on trial in his killing, an investigator testified Thursday. Sheldon “Timothy” Herrington Jr.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NASA's stuck astronauts hit 6 months in space. Just 2 more to go
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — Known across the globe as the stuck astronauts, Butch Wilmore and Suni Williams hit the six-month mark in space Thursday with two more to go.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Anthem Blue Cross Blue Shield reverses decision to put a time limit on anesthesia
item.description: One of the country’s largest health insurers reversed a change in policy Thursday after widespread outcry, saying it would not tie payments in some states to the length of time a patient went under anesthesia.
item.dc:creator: DEVNA BOSE AP Health Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NASA pushes back astronaut flights to the moon again
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — NASA announced more delays Thursday in sending astronauts back to the moon more than 50 years after Apollo. Administrator Bill Nelson said the next mission in the Artemis program -- flying four astronauts around the moon and back – is now targeted for April 2026.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Link to adult website on Mattel's 'Wicked' dolls at center of proposed class action lawsuit
item.description: A mother claims she and her daughter visited the link on the box, only to be "horrified" by what they saw, according to the lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: QVC recalls 1.1 million oven gloves after more than 90 burn injury reports
item.description: QVC is recalling more than 1 million pairs of oven gloves after dozens of people were burned while using them, the U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission has announced.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democratic rep pushes Biden to issue blanket pardon to those Patel could target
item.description: He noted that law enforcement officers, military personnel and others are at risk of extreme legal battles.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump assassination attempt: Secret Service acting director reveals 'numerous changes' agency has implemented
item.description: Acting Secret Service Director Ronald Rowe revealed the "numerous changes" the agency implemented following the attempted assassination attempt of President-elect Trump in Butler.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gunman who shot 2 kindergartners at a California school wrote about attack targeting children
item.description: A gunman who critically wounded two kindergartners at a tiny religious school in Northern California was mentally ill and believed by targeting children he was carrying out “counter-measures” in response to America’s involvement in Middle East violence.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: New clues emerge as investigators hunt for the gunman who killed UnitedHealthcare's CEO
item.description: New clues are emerging in the hunt for the masked gunman who stalked and killed UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, including possible leads about his travel before the shooting and a message scrawled on ammunition found at the crime scene.
item.dc:creator: Jake Offenhartz, Karen Matthews And Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Union rights take center stage in already high-stakes Wisconsin Supreme Court race
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Wisconsin's state Supreme Court election next spring already had high stakes, with majority control on the line.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Stadium-size asteroid to make closest approach to Earth
item.description: The stadium-size asteroid is considered a "potentially hazardous object," but poses no threat to Earth this year.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: A list of mispronounced words provides a retrospective of 2024, from Kamala to Chappell
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris and breakout pop star Chappell Roan were among the year's most talked-about people. Their names were also among the most mispronounced.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Daily walks can add years to your life, says new study
item.description: Check out fitness trackers, running shoes, workout clothes and more to make your fitness journey as easy and comfortable as it can possibly be.
item.dc:creator: Christina Marfice, Fox 5

item.title: Billionaire who performed the first private spacewalk is Trump's pick to lead NASA
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump has tapped a tech billionaire who performed the first private spacewalk to lead NASA. Trump announced on Wednesday his choice for NASA administrator.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN AP Aerospace Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump’s pick to run the DEA withdraws his name from consideration. Trump says he pulled the plug
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Chad Chronister, Donald Trump's pick to run the Drug Enforcement Administration, has withdrawn his name from consideration for the job, becoming the second person selected by the president-elect to bow out quickly after being selected for a position requiring Senate confirmation.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Max to begin cracking down on password sharing next week
item.description: Max will begin its password-sharing crackdown next week, beginning with "very early, gentle messaging" directed at high-usage users.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, ABC 4

item.title: No winner for Mega Millions, jackpot grows to $579 million
item.description: No one won the Mega Millions drawing on Tuesday night. The jackpot has now grown to an estimated $579 million ahead of the next drawing on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Knight, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Here's the top 25 list of most-viewed Wikipedia articles of 2024
item.description: We collectively spent an estimated 2.4 billion hours reading English-language articles on Wikipedia in 2024, and we now know which were the most popular.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Adams, ABC 4
